articles.title: Rachael Rollins, former U.S. Attorney who resigned amid ethics scandal, reportedly running again for Suffolk DA
articles.description:
Rollins previously served as Suffolk County’ District Attorney from 2019 to 2021, when she was appointed to be U.S. Attorney.

The post Rachael Rollins, former U.S. Attorney who resigned amid ethics scandal, reportedly running again for Suffolk DA appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Rachael Rollins announces candidacy for Suffolk District Attorney, aiming to reclaim her former position.
Rollins previously served as Suffolk DA from 2019 to 2021 before becoming U.S. Attorney.
Her return could influence local criminal justice policies and community safety in Boston.

articles.title: Firefighters ask for support for colleague critically injured in training academy fall
articles.description:
The union is asking for community support for the young firefighter, who will need multiple surgeries to fully recover.

The post Firefighters ask for support for colleague critically injured in training academy fall appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Firefighter critically injured during training academy fall requires multiple surgeries for recovery.
The union is seeking community support for the injured firefighter's medical expenses.
Local residents can contribute to aid the firefighter's recovery and support his family.

articles.title: How an old Roman Anthony TikTok video inspired Red Sox’ new celebration
articles.description:
"Now I’ve gotta explain to my boys what that is all about."

The post How an old Roman Anthony TikTok video inspired Red Sox’ new celebration appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Red Sox adopt TikTok-inspired celebration from Roman Anthony's viral video.
Roman Anthony, a Boston native, gained popularity with his dance on TikTok.
New celebration aims to engage younger fans and boost local team spirit.

articles.title: Former Mass. police chief charged with committing fraud while leading department
articles.description:
The former chief allegedly made fraudulent claims for nearly $20,000 in overtime pay that he was not eligible for.

The post Former Mass. police chief charged with committing fraud while leading department appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Former police chief charged with fraud for claiming $20,000 in ineligible overtime pay.
The alleged fraud occurred while he was leading the department in Massachusetts.
This case raises concerns about accountability in local law enforcement for Boston residents.

articles.title: North Andover police unions call for ‘immediate removal’ of chief after Fitzsimmons acquittal
articles.description:
“Chief Gray has proven unable to fulfill the basic qualifications of an effective leader. Unfortunately, the consequences are landing squarely on our officers and our Town.”

The post North Andover police unions call for ‘immediate removal’ of chief after Fitzsimmons acquittal appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: North Andover police unions demand Chief Gray's removal following Fitzsimmons' acquittal on April 8.
Unions claim Gray lacks basic leadership qualifications, affecting officer morale and community trust.
Immediate leadership changes could influence police operations and public safety in North Andover.
